			<script type="text/rocketlazyloadscript" data-rocket-type="text/javascript">
			window.flatStyles = window.flatStyles || ''

			window.lightspeedOptimizeStylesheet = function () {
				const currentStylesheet = document.querySelector( '.tcb-lightspeed-style:not([data-ls-optimized])' )

				if ( currentStylesheet ) {
					try {
						if ( currentStylesheet.sheet && currentStylesheet.sheet.cssRules ) {
							if ( window.flatStyles ) {
								if ( this.optimizing ) {
									setTimeout( window.lightspeedOptimizeStylesheet.bind( this ), 24 )
								} else {
									this.optimizing = true;

									let rulesIndex = 0;

									while ( rulesIndex < currentStylesheet.sheet.cssRules.length ) {
										const rule = currentStylesheet.sheet.cssRules[ rulesIndex ]
										/* remove rules that already exist in the page */
										if ( rule.type === CSSRule.STYLE_RULE && window.flatStyles.includes( `${rule.selectorText}{` ) ) {
											currentStylesheet.sheet.deleteRule( rulesIndex )
										} else {
											rulesIndex ++
										}
									}
									/* optimize, mark it such, move to the next file, append the styles we have until now */
									currentStylesheet.setAttribute( 'data-ls-optimized', '1' )

									window.flatStyles += currentStylesheet.innerHTML

									this.optimizing = false
								}
							} else {
								window.flatStyles = currentStylesheet.innerHTML
								currentStylesheet.setAttribute( 'data-ls-optimized', '1' )
							}
						}
					} catch ( error ) {
						console.warn( error )
					}

					if ( currentStylesheet.parentElement.tagName !== 'HEAD' ) {
						/* always make sure that those styles end up in the head */
						const stylesheetID = currentStylesheet.id;
						/**
						 * make sure that there is only one copy of the css
						 * e.g display CSS
						 */
						if ( ( ! stylesheetID || ( stylesheetID && ! document.querySelector( `head #${stylesheetID}` ) ) ) ) {
							document.head.prepend( currentStylesheet )
						} else {
							currentStylesheet.remove();
						}
					}
				}
			}

			window.lightspeedOptimizeFlat = function ( styleSheetElement ) {
				if ( document.querySelectorAll( 'link[href*="thrive_flat.css"]' ).length > 1 ) {
					/* disable this flat if we already have one */
					styleSheetElement.setAttribute( 'disabled', true )
				} else {
					/* if this is the first one, make sure he's in head */
					if ( styleSheetElement.parentElement.tagName !== 'HEAD' ) {
						document.head.append( styleSheetElement )
					}
				}
			}
		</script>

<style type="text/css" id="thrive-default-styles"></style><script type="application/ld+json" class="kadence-faq-schema-graph kadence-faq-schema-graph--kb-faq5396_76aacc-50">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name": "Is there a free plan?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type": "Answer","text": "<p> No. HighLevel is paid, with a standard 14-day free trial period. The good news: there’s an <strong><a>official 30-day promo</a></strong> for <strong>Starter</strong> and <strong>Unlimited,</strong> so you can set things up properly. If you want to test <strong>SaaS Mode</strong>, use <strong><a>my special promo link</a></strong> to get a <strong>30-day Pro/SaaS trial</strong>.</p>"}},{"@type":"Question","name": "Is there a GoHighLevel lifetime deal?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type": "Answer","text": "<p>There isn’t an official LTD. Plans are monthly/annual. However, I track promos and debunk myths here: <a>GoHighLevel lifetime deal</a> and <a>GoHighLevel Black Friday</a><a> deals</a>.</p>"}},{"@type":"Question","name": "Do I have to pay extra for SMS/voice/email?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type": "Answer","text": "<p> Yes. Plan fees cover the platform. <strong>LC Phone</strong> (numbers, SMS/MMS, call minutes), <strong>A2P/10DLC</strong> (US), and <strong>LC Email</strong> sending/verification are usage‑based.</p>"}},{"@type":"Question","name": "What are the current US usage rates?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type": "Answer","text": "<p> Common ballpark: SMS ~$0.0079/segment, MMS ~$0.02 outbound, outbound calls ~$0.018/min, inbound calls ~$0.0085/min, email sending $0.675/1,000, verification $2.50/1,000. International rates vary.</p>"}},{"@type":"Question","name": "<strong>Can I bring my own Twilio/Mailgun?</strong>","acceptedAnswer":{"@type": "Answer","text": "<p> Yes, but most agencies prefer <strong>LeadConnector (LC)</strong> because it supports rebilling and keeps setup simpler. If you BYO, you’ll manage billing separately and lose some rebilling convenience.</p>"}},{"@type":"Question","name": "<strong>Does Starter really include unlimited contacts & users?</strong>","acceptedAnswer":{"@type": "Answer","text": "<p> Yes. The cap is on <strong>sub‑accounts (up to 3)</strong>, not contacts or seats.</p>"}},{"@type":"Question","name": "<strong>Is the HIPAA add‑on worth it?</strong>","acceptedAnswer":{"@type": "Answer","text": "<p>Only if you handle ePHI. It’s <strong>$297/mo</strong> (or <strong>$2,970/yr</strong>) and once enabled, <strong>you can’t remove it</strong>.</p>"}},{"@type":"Question","name": "How does the AI Employee billing work?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type": "Answer","text": "<p>You can choose <strong>$97/mo unlimited</strong> (per sub‑account) or pay‑as‑you‑go. Agencies on Pro can package and mark this up.</p>"}},{"@type":"Question","name": "<strong>Does HighLevel offer annual discounts?</strong>","acceptedAnswer":{"@type": "Answer","text": "<p>Yes — typically <strong>~2 months free</strong> when you pay annually (see plan table above).</p>"}},{"@type":"Question","name": "<strong>Can I resell WordPress hosting?</strong>","acceptedAnswer":{"@type": "Answer","text": "<p>Yes. Standard is <strong>$10/site/mo</strong>. There are higher tiers for agencies with lots of sites.</p>"}},{"@type":"Question","name": "<strong>Refunds?</strong>","acceptedAnswer":{"@type": "Answer","text": "<p>HighLevel trials are free; paid plans generally renew monthly/annually. Always cancel before renewal if you’re not continuing.</p>"}}]}</script><link rel="icon" href="https://passivesecrets.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/cropped-passivesecrets-gear-only-favicon-min-32x32.png" sizes="32x32">

				<h1 class="entry-title" itemprop="headline">GoHighLevel Pricing (2025): Plans, Real Costs & Hidden Fees [Ultimate Guide]</h1>		<div class="entry-meta">
			<span class="posted-on"><time class="entry-date published" datetime="2025-10-09T18:38:38-04:00" itemprop="datePublished">October 9, 2025</time></span> <span class="byline">by <span class="author vcard" itemprop="author" itemtype="https://schema.org/Person" itemscope=""><a class="url fn n" href="https://passivesecrets.com/author/valentine-okoronkwo/" title="View all posts by Valentine Okoronkwo" rel="author" itemprop="url"><span class="author-name" itemprop="name">Valentine Okoronkwo</span></a></span></span> 		</div>

			<div class="kb-row-layout-wrap kb-row-layout-id5396_f9b04c-4f alignnone wp-block-kadence-rowlayout"><div class="kt-row-column-wrap kt-has-1-columns kt-row-layout-equal kt-tab-layout-inherit kt-mobile-layout-row kt-row-valign-top">

<div class="wp-block-kadence-column kadence-column5396_c3ae94-d8"><div class="kt-inside-inner-col">
<h2 class="wp-block-heading">TL;DR (GoHighLevel Pricing)</h2>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>Starter — $97/month</strong>: unlimited contacts & users, <strong>up to 3 sub‑accounts</strong>. Great for solo operators and small teams.</li>



<li><strong>Unlimited — $297/month</strong>: everything in Starter <strong>+ unlimited sub‑accounts</strong>, API access, branded desktop app.</li>



<li><strong>Pro/SaaS — $497/month</strong>: unlock SaaS Mode, advanced rebilling, client billing automation. Best when you want to sell HighLevel as your own software.</li>



<li><strong>Annual billing (optional)</strong>: save ~<strong>2 months</strong> — $970/yr, $2,970/yr, $4,970/yr.</li>



<li><span style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px;"><strong>Usage-based costs</strong> still apply<span style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px;">: SMS/voice via <strong>LC Phone,</strong> email sends/veri</span>fication via LC Email, A2P/10DLC fees for US messaging, WordPress hosting, optional HIPAA, and optional AI Employee at $97/month (<strong>unlimited</strong> or pay-as-you-go</span>).</li>



<li><strong>Extended free trial available</strong>: Standard access starts with a 14-day free trial. <span style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px;">However, you can get an extended <strong>30-day</strong> trial via official promos</span>.</li>
</ul>

<p><a href="https://passivesecrets.com/what-is-gohighlevel/">GoHighLevel</a> is like the Swiss Army Knife of digital marketing tools. It has features that cover almost every aspect of digital marketing. But how much does it cost?</p>



<p>There are rumors that GoHighLevel has some hidden costs, and the more features are added, the higher the pricing tiers become.</p>



<p>To answer these questions and more you may have, this article fully explains GoHighLevel pricing plans, the secret costs, and the features you’ll get on each plan. It also compares the pricing plans with some top alternatives. </p>



<p>Keep reading to know which plan is best for you and if it is worth it. </p>

<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Hidden costs & usage‑based charges</h2>



<p>One major con about GHL is that the pricing isn’t always straightforward. HighLevel offers some services and add-on features that are separate from the regular features on any tier plan you choose. </p>



<p>These extra services and add-ons are charged at different fees. </p>



<p>As a frequent GHL user, I am aware of all the fees, and I have listed and explained them. Below is a <strong>beginner-friendly ledger</strong> of what can add to your bill and how to think about it.</p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>1) LC Phone (SMS/MMS/Voice)</strong></h3>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>What it is:</strong> HighLevel’s built‑in telephony (numbers, SMS/MMS, inbound/outbound calls). Pricing mirrors the underlying carrier (commonly Twilio‑equivalent in the US/CA) + <strong>A2P/10DLC</strong> compliance fees for US messaging.</li>



<li><strong>What to expect:</strong> Pay per number/month, per SMS/MMS segment, and per call minute. A2P has one‑time registration + monthly campaign fees, plus carrier message fees.</li>



<li><strong>Agency tip:</strong> Enable rebilling (see next section) so client usage funds your wallet automatically. Rates track <strong><a href="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/voice/pricing/us" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener nofollow">typical carrier passthrough fees</a></strong>, which can change—budget with a small buffer.</li>
</ul>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>2) LC Email — sending</strong></h3>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>What it is:</strong> Built‑in SMTP for transactional/marketing emails.</li>



<li><strong>Typical cost:</strong> <strong>$0.675 per 1,000 emails</strong> sent.</li>



<li><strong>Agency tip:</strong> Keep an eye on sub‑accounts with heavy sending; warm up domains properly to protect deliverability. Follow <strong><a href="https://gmail.com/postmaster/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener nofollow">sender reputation best practices</a></strong> to keep deliverability strong as volume grows.</li>
</ul>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>3) LC Email — verification</strong></h3>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>What it is:</strong> Optional verification to reduce bounces.</li>



<li><strong>Typical cost:</strong> <strong>$2.50 per 1,000</strong> verifications.</li>



<li><strong>Agency tip:</strong> Rebill verification to clients (Unlimited/Pro) so heavy list cleaning doesn’t eat your margins. </li>
</ul>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>4) A2P/10DLC (US texting only)</strong></h3>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>What it is:</strong> Required registration/vetting for US business texting, plus monthly campaign fees and carrier per‑message surcharges. See the <strong><a href="https://www.campaignregistry.com/" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener">official 10DLC overview</a></strong> for current registration and campaign guidance.</li>



<li><strong>What to expect:</strong> One‑time brand/campaign vetting + ongoing campaign fees. Per‑message carrier fees apply on top of SMS segment pricing.</li>



<li><strong>Agency tip:</strong> Build this into your client onboarding and pricing. It reduces deliverability headaches and protects your numbers.</li>
</ul>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>5) WordPress Hosting (optional)</strong></h3>



<figure class="wp-block-kadence-image kb-image5396_a10acd-97 size-large"><a href="https://passivesecrets.com/recommends/ghl-wordpress-hosting/pricing" class="kb-advanced-image-link" target="_blank" <li><strong>Typical cost:</strong> <strong>Standard $10/mo</strong> (1 site). Higher‑tier bundles are available if you host lots of sites.</li>



<li><strong>Agency tip:</strong> Productize this in your plans (e.g., “Pro” package includes a WP site). Easy recurring revenue.</li>
</ul>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>6) HIPAA add‑on</strong></h3>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>What it is:</strong> Account‑wide HIPAA compliance (encryption, BAA, audit logging, MFA enforcement).</li>



<li><strong>Cost:</strong> <strong>$297/mo</strong> or <strong>$2,970/yr</strong> (two months free). <strong>Irreversible</strong> once enabled. Review the <strong><a href="https://www.hhs.gov/hipaa/for-professionals/privacy/laws-regulations/index.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener">HIPAA Security Rule basics</a></strong> to decide if you truly need this add-on. </li>



<li><strong>Agency tip:</strong> Only add if you truly handle electronic protected health information (ePHI). Price your packages accordingly. </li>
</ul>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>7) AI Employee</strong></h3>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>What it is:</strong> <a href="https://passivesecrets.com/recommends/ghl-ai-employee/pricing" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener nofollow">A bundle of AI tools</a> (Conversation AI, Voice AI, Reviews AI, Content AI, Workflow AI, etc.).</li>



<li><strong>Pricing options:</strong> <strong>$97/mo unlimited</strong> <strong>per sub‑account</strong>, <strong>or</strong> pay‑as‑you‑go.</li>



<li><strong>Agency tip:</strong> Great for “we answer every call/chat” outcomes. Resell at a profit on Pro.</li>
</ul>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>8) Domains, marketplace apps, extras</strong></h3>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>Domains:</strong> You can enable domain purchase & rebill. Prices vary by TLD.</li>



<li><strong>App Marketplace:</strong> Third‑party and HighLevel apps may be free, fixed‑fee, or usage‑based. Many are resellable.</li>
</ul>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>9) LC Premium Triggers & Actions (Workflows)</strong></h3>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>What it is:</strong> Certain Workflow Triggers/Actions are marked <strong>Premium</strong> (e.g., inbound webhooks, some integrations, select AI steps). These incur a small <strong>per‑execution</strong> fee unless covered by a specific tier.</li>



<li><strong>How it’s billed:</strong> You pay per execution. Agencies can opt into a <strong>Workflow Pro</strong> tier for bundled executions/discounted overages. Some Marketplace apps may intermittently <strong>waive</strong> the HighLevel premium portion as part of official experiments. New/existing sub‑accounts often receive a small <strong>free execution</strong> allotment when Premium is enabled.</li>



<li><strong>Agency tip:</strong> On <strong>Unlimited/Pro</strong>, enable <strong>rebilling</strong> so Premium executions pass through (or include a margin) instead of hitting your wallet.</li>
</ul>



<h3 class="kt-adv-heading5396_3615ba-18 wp-block-kadence-advancedheading" data-kb-block="kb-adv-heading5396_3615ba-18">Add‑On Services</h3>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>Basic / Advanced Account Setup:</strong> One‑time implementation packages for faster launch (onboarding, snapshots, best‑practice wiring). Good when time is scarce.</li>



<li><strong><a href="https://passivesecrets.com/recommends/ghl-premium-support/pricing" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener nofollow">Priority Support</a>:</strong> An optional monthly upgrade for faster response SLAs.</li>



<li><strong>Eliza Agent Platform:</strong> A separate, HighLevel‑run service focused on lead answering & nurturing to bookings; billed monthly with a setup fee.</li>



<li><strong><a href="https://passivesecrets.com/recommends/ghl-white-label-mobile-app/pricing" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener nofollow">White‑Label Mobile App</a>:</strong> Publish your <strong>own‑branded</strong> iOS/Android app for client access (additional fee; typically available to Unlimited/Pro agencies).</li>



<li><strong>White‑Label Zap (Custom Zap)</strong>: Branded Zapier connector for your SaaS offer (additional fee).</li>



<li><strong>Ads Manager:</strong> Optional in‑platform <strong>Meta/Google</strong> ads tool with agency‑side pricing models (per sub‑account or unlimited tiers) and the ability to resell to clients.</li>
</ul>



<p>These add‑ons are optional. Use only if they support your model (e.g., SaaS offer, done‑for‑you service, or higher‑touch support).</p>



<div style="height:1px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) examples</h2>



<p>It’s obvious that HighLevel’s flat plan fee is only part of the story. You’ll also have usage costs and optional add‑ons. Below are <strong>realistic example scenarios</strong> using commonly seen US rates.</p>



<p><strong>Assumptions (illustrative, US/Canada):</strong></p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>SMS</strong>: ~$0.0079 per segment (in/out). 1 message can be 1–3 segments depending on length.</li>



<li><strong>Voice</strong>: Outbound ~$0.018/min; inbound ~$0.0085/min.</li>



<li><strong>Email sending</strong>: <strong>$0.675 per 1,000</strong> emails.</li>



<li><strong>Email verification</strong>: <strong>$2.50 per 1,000</strong> verifications.</li>



<li><strong>WordPress hosting (Standard)</strong>: <strong>$10/site/mo</strong>.</li>



<li><strong>AI Employee (optional)</strong>: <strong>$97/mo</strong> for unlimited usage <strong>per sub‑account</strong>, or pay‑as‑you‑go.</li>



<li><strong>A2P/10DLC (US texting)</strong>: registration/vetting + monthly campaign fees apply; carrier passthrough fees are per message.</li>



<li>Taxes/marketplace extras not included. International rates vary.</li>
</ul>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>Scenario A — Solo brand on Starter</strong></h3>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>1 sub‑account (your business)</li>



<li>3,000 emails/month → <strong>$2.03</strong></li>



<li>800 SMS segments/month → <strong>$6.32</strong></li>



<li>60 min outbound + 40 min inbound calls → <strong>$2.20</strong></li>



<li>Email verification (2,000/month) → <strong>$5.00</strong></li>



<li>No WordPress hosting, no AI Employee</li>
</ul>



<p><strong>Estimated monthly</strong>: $97 plan + $2.03 + $6.32 + $2.20 + $5.00 ≈ <strong>$112.55</strong></p>



<h3 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>Scenario B — Growing agency on Unlimited</strong></h3>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>3 sub‑accounts (you + 2 clients)</li>



<li>Per month, combined: 25,000 emails → <strong>$16.88</strong></li>



<li>8,000 SMS segments → <strong>$63.20</strong></li>



<li>500 min outbound + 300 min inbound → <strong>$15.30</strong></li>



<li>Email verification (10,000/month) → <strong>$25.00</strong></li>



<li>WordPress hosting for 2 client sites → <strong>$20.00</strong></li>



<li>AI Employee on 1 client account (flat) → <strong>$97.00</strong></li>
</ul>



<p><strong>Estimated monthly</strong>: $297 plan + $16.88 + $63.20 + $15.30 + $25.00 + $20.00 + $97 ≈ <strong>$534.38</strong></p>


<div class="kb-row-layout-wrap kb-row-layout-id5396_ee2342-cd alignnone wp-block-kadence-rowlayout"><div class="kt-row-column-wrap kt-has-1-columns kt-row-layout-equal kt-tab-layout-inherit kt-mobile-layout-row kt-row-valign-top">

<div class="wp-block-kadence-column kadence-column5396_b828e5-53 inner-column-1"><div class="kt-inside-inner-col">
<p><strong>Why agencies love the Unlimited plan:</strong> You get unlimited sub‑accounts, so adding clients doesn’t increase your base software fee. Your variable costs scale with actual usage.</p>
</div></div>

</div></div>


<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Rebilling: who can charge what back to clients?</h2>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>Starter ($97)</strong> — designed for a single brand; <strong>no</strong> advanced rebilling controls.</li>



<li><strong>Unlimited ($297)</strong> — basic rebilling on select services (e.g., fixed markup on some items like email verification). Great for pass‑through of usage to clients.</li>



<li><strong>Pro/SaaS ($497)</strong> — full <strong>SaaS Mode</strong> pricing + <strong>flexible markups</strong>, automated client billing, dunning, and plan packaging.</li>
</ul>


<div class="kb-row-layout-wrap kb-row-layout-id5396_2e97b1-0c alignnone wp-block-kadence-rowlayout"><div class="kt-row-column-wrap kt-has-1-columns kt-row-layout-equal kt-tab-layout-inherit kt-mobile-layout-row kt-row-valign-top">

<div class="wp-block-kadence-column kadence-column5396_3eaa29-b0 inner-column-1"><div class="kt-inside-inner-col">
<p class="kt-adv-heading5396_7a375f-2f wp-block-kadence-advancedheading" data-kb-block="kb-adv-heading5396_7a375f-2f"><strong>Practical tip:</strong> On Unlimited/Pro, turn on rebilling the <strong>moment</strong> you onboard a client. Map every usage‑based item (LC Phone, LC Email sending/verification, WordPress, AI Employee, domains) to your client plan with either pass‑through or a margin.</p>
</div></div>

</div></div>

<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Which plan should you pick? (by scenario)</h2>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>“It’s just me (or my small team)” → Starter</strong>
<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>You’ll get unlimited contacts/users and up to 3 sub‑accounts (enough for your brand + one sandbox + a test client).</li>



<li>Add LC Email verification early; your deliverability will thank you.</li>
</ul>
</li>



<li><strong>“I’m an agency with multiple clients” → Unlimited</strong>
<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>Unlimited sub‑accounts removes the ceiling on growth.</li>



<li>Turn on rebilling so your wallet never becomes a surprise expense.</li>
</ul>
</li>



<li><strong>“I want to sell GHL as my own software” → Pro/SaaS mode</strong>
<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li>Productize plans, set your own prices, and bundle usage (SMS, email, AI) with markups.</li>



<li>Add‑on the branded mobile app if you need app‑store presence.</li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading">Upgrade/downgrade paths that save money</h2>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>Path 1 (popular):</strong> Start on <strong>Unlimited</strong> to get everything you need for an agency. If you only keep your own brand after 1–2 months, <strong>downgrade to Starter</strong>.</li>



<li><strong>Path 2 (SaaS path):</strong> Validate offers on <strong>Unlimited</strong>, then move to <strong>Pro/SaaS</strong> when you’re ready to sell plans at scale with automated rebilling.</li>



<li><strong>Extended trial (via my link):</strong> Start on <strong>Unlimited</strong>/<strong>Pro</strong> to get <strong>up to 30 days</strong>, then downgrade to <strong>Starter</strong> before the trial ends if you want the $97 plan. Access details in the final CTA below.</li>
</ul>

<li><strong>2023–2024:</strong> A2P/10DLC enforcement tightens in the US. Rebilling expands; Marketplace grows.</li>



<li><strong>2024:</strong> WordPress hosting added/expanded; agencies begin reselling sites inside GHL.</li>



<li><strong>2025:</strong> <strong>AI Employee</strong> introduced/standardized with a <strong>$97/mo unlimited</strong> option alongside usage‑based pricing. WordPress and HIPAA pricing clarified with annual‑save‑two‑months options.</li>
</ul>

<h2 class="wp-block-heading">FAQs</h2>



<div class="wp-block-kadence-accordion alignnone"><div class="kt-accordion-wrap kt-accordion-id5396_76aacc-50 kt-accordion-has-14-panes kt-active-pane-0 kt-accordion-block kt-pane-header-alignment-left kt-accodion-icon-style-basic kt-accodion-icon-side-right" style="max-width:none"><div class="kt-accordion-inner-wrap" data-allow-multiple-open="false" data-start-open="0">
<div class="wp-block-kadence-pane kt-accordion-pane kt-accordion-pane-5 kt-pane5396_d52c43-60"><h3 class="kt-accordion-header-wrap"><button class="kt-blocks-accordion-header kt-acccordion-button-label-show" type="button"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title-wrap"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title">Is there a free plan?</span></span><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-icon-trigger"></span></button></h3><div class="kt-accordion-panel kt-accordion-panel-hidden"><div class="kt-accordion-panel-inner">
<p class="kt-adv-heading5396_9f7e1b-89 wp-block-kadence-advancedheading" data-kb-block="kb-adv-heading5396_9f7e1b-89"> No. HighLevel is paid, with a standard <b>14-day free trial period</b>. The good news: there’s an <strong><a href="https://passivesecrets.com/recommends/ghl-30day-trial/pricing" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer noopener nofollow">official 30-day promo</a></strong> for <strong>Starter</strong> and <strong>Unlimited,</strong> so you can set things up properly. If you want to test <strong>SaaS Mode</strong>, use <strong><a href="https://passivesecrets.com/recommends/ghl-30-day-trial/pricing">my special promo link</a></strong> to get a <strong>30-day Pro/SaaS trial</strong>.</p>
</div></div></div>



<div class="wp-block-kadence-pane kt-accordion-pane kt-accordion-pane-20 kt-pane5396_cc7011-53"><h3 class="kt-accordion-header-wrap"><button class="kt-blocks-accordion-header kt-acccordion-button-label-show" type="button"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title-wrap"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title">Is there a GoHighLevel lifetime deal?</span></span><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-icon-trigger"></span></button></h3><div class="kt-accordion-panel kt-accordion-panel-hidden"><div class="kt-accordion-panel-inner">
<p>There isn’t an official LTD. Plans are monthly/annual. However, I track promos and <span style="box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0px; padding: 0px;">debunk myths here: <a href="https://passivesecrets.com/gohighlevel-lifetime-deal/" target="_blank">GoHighLevel lifetime deal</a> and <a href="https://passivesecrets.com/gohighlevel-black-friday-deal/">GoHighLevel Black Friday</a></span><a href="https://passivesecrets.com/gohighlevel-discount-coupon-code/"> deals</a>.</p>
</div></div></div>



<div class="wp-block-kadence-pane kt-accordion-pane kt-accordion-pane-1 kt-pane5396_8ed907-7e"><h3 class="kt-accordion-header-wrap"><button class="kt-blocks-accordion-header kt-acccordion-button-label-show" type="button"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title-wrap"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title">Do I have to pay extra for SMS/voice/email?</span></span><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-icon-trigger"></span></button></h3><div class="kt-accordion-panel kt-accordion-panel-hidden"><div class="kt-accordion-panel-inner">
<p class="kt-adv-heading5396_14d511-d6 wp-block-kadence-advancedheading" data-kb-block="kb-adv-heading5396_14d511-d6"> Yes. Plan fees cover the platform. <strong>LC Phone</strong> (numbers, SMS/MMS, call minutes), <strong>A2P/10DLC</strong> (US), and <strong>LC Email</strong> sending/verification are usage‑based.</p>
</div></div></div>



<div class="wp-block-kadence-pane kt-accordion-pane kt-accordion-pane-6 kt-pane5396_eae0d3-e2"><h3 class="kt-accordion-header-wrap"><button class="kt-blocks-accordion-header kt-acccordion-button-label-show" type="button"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title-wrap"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title">What are the current US usage rates?</span></span><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-icon-trigger"></span></button></h3><div class="kt-accordion-panel kt-accordion-panel-hidden"><div class="kt-accordion-panel-inner">
<p class="kt-adv-heading5396_a0e2a3-9f wp-block-kadence-advancedheading" data-kb-block="kb-adv-heading5396_a0e2a3-9f"> Common ballpark: SMS ~$0.0079/segment, MMS ~$0.02 outbound, outbound calls ~$0.018/min, inbound calls ~$0.0085/min, email sending $0.675/1,000, verification $2.50/1,000. International rates vary.</p>
</div></div></div>



<div class="wp-block-kadence-pane kt-accordion-pane kt-accordion-pane-7 kt-pane5396_c87a60-a1"><h3 class="kt-accordion-header-wrap"><button class="kt-blocks-accordion-header kt-acccordion-button-label-show" type="button"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title-wrap"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title"><strong>Can I bring my own Twilio/Mailgun?</strong></span></span><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-icon-trigger"></span></button></h3><div class="kt-accordion-panel kt-accordion-panel-hidden"><div class="kt-accordion-panel-inner">
<p class="kt-adv-heading5396_24d3e2-63 wp-block-kadence-advancedheading" data-kb-block="kb-adv-heading5396_24d3e2-63"> Yes, but most agencies prefer <strong>LeadConnector (LC)</strong> because it supports rebilling and keeps setup simpler. If you BYO, you’ll manage billing separately and lose some rebilling convenience.</p>
</div></div></div>



<div class="wp-block-kadence-pane kt-accordion-pane kt-accordion-pane-8 kt-pane5396_a3f4ae-1b"><h3 class="kt-accordion-header-wrap"><button class="kt-blocks-accordion-header kt-acccordion-button-label-show" type="button"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title-wrap"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title"><strong>Does Starter really include unlimited contacts & users?</strong></span></span><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-icon-trigger"></span></button></h3><div class="kt-accordion-panel kt-accordion-panel-hidden"><div class="kt-accordion-panel-inner">
<p class="kt-adv-heading5396_082c0d-2a wp-block-kadence-advancedheading" data-kb-block="kb-adv-heading5396_082c0d-2a"> Yes. The cap is on <strong>sub‑accounts (up to 3)</strong>, not contacts or seats.</p>
</div></div></div>



<div class="wp-block-kadence-pane kt-accordion-pane kt-accordion-pane-9 kt-pane5396_dc6ee5-fc"><h3 class="kt-accordion-header-wrap"><button class="kt-blocks-accordion-header kt-acccordion-button-label-show" type="button"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title-wrap"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title"><strong>Is the HIPAA add‑on worth it?</strong></span></span><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-icon-trigger"></span></button></h3><div class="kt-accordion-panel kt-accordion-panel-hidden"><div class="kt-accordion-panel-inner">
<p class="kt-adv-heading5396_4bee25-bb wp-block-kadence-advancedheading" data-kb-block="kb-adv-heading5396_4bee25-bb">Only if you handle ePHI. It’s <strong>$297/mo</strong> (or <strong>$2,970/yr</strong>) and once enabled, <strong>you can’t remove it</strong>.</p>
</div></div></div>



<div class="wp-block-kadence-pane kt-accordion-pane kt-accordion-pane-10 kt-pane5396_982317-6c"><h3 class="kt-accordion-header-wrap"><button class="kt-blocks-accordion-header kt-acccordion-button-label-show" type="button"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title-wrap"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title">How does the AI Employee billing work?</span></span><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-icon-trigger"></span></button></h3><div class="kt-accordion-panel kt-accordion-panel-hidden"><div class="kt-accordion-panel-inner">
<p class="kt-adv-heading5396_78c822-6d wp-block-kadence-advancedheading" data-kb-block="kb-adv-heading5396_78c822-6d">You can choose <strong>$97/mo unlimited</strong> (per sub‑account) or pay‑as‑you‑go. Agencies on Pro can package and mark this up.</p>
</div></div></div>



<div class="wp-block-kadence-pane kt-accordion-pane kt-accordion-pane-11 kt-pane5396_a95aa2-f4"><h3 class="kt-accordion-header-wrap"><button class="kt-blocks-accordion-header kt-acccordion-button-label-show" type="button"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title-wrap"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title"><strong>Does HighLevel offer annual discounts?</strong></span></span><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-icon-trigger"></span></button></h3><div class="kt-accordion-panel kt-accordion-panel-hidden"><div class="kt-accordion-panel-inner">
<p class="kt-adv-heading5396_2fa201-b7 wp-block-kadence-advancedheading" data-kb-block="kb-adv-heading5396_2fa201-b7">Yes — typically <strong>~2 months free</strong> when you pay annually (see plan table above).</p>
</div></div></div>



<div class="wp-block-kadence-pane kt-accordion-pane kt-accordion-pane-12 kt-pane5396_cb0fc7-b3"><h3 class="kt-accordion-header-wrap"><button class="kt-blocks-accordion-header kt-acccordion-button-label-show" type="button"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title-wrap"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title"><strong>Can I resell WordPress hosting?</strong></span></span><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-icon-trigger"></span></button></h3><div class="kt-accordion-panel kt-accordion-panel-hidden"><div class="kt-accordion-panel-inner">
<p class="kt-adv-heading5396_4f6381-07 wp-block-kadence-advancedheading" data-kb-block="kb-adv-heading5396_4f6381-07">Yes. Standard is <strong>$10/site/mo</strong>. There are higher tiers for agencies with lots of sites.</p>
</div></div></div>



<div class="wp-block-kadence-pane kt-accordion-pane kt-accordion-pane-13 kt-pane5396_da5b79-ec"><h3 class="kt-accordion-header-wrap"><button class="kt-blocks-accordion-header kt-acccordion-button-label-show" type="button"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title-wrap"><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-title"><strong>Refunds?</strong></span></span><span class="kt-blocks-accordion-icon-trigger"></span></button></h3><div class="kt-accordion-panel kt-accordion-panel-hidden"><div class="kt-accordion-panel-inner">
<p class="kt-adv-heading5396_f97173-5c wp-block-kadence-advancedheading" data-kb-block="kb-adv-heading5396_f97173-5c">HighLevel trials are free; paid plans generally renew monthly/annually. Always cancel before renewal if you’re not continuing.</p>
</div></div></div>
</div></div></div>



<div style="height:1px" aria-hidden="true" class="wp-block-spacer"></div>



<h2 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>Conclusion: Is GHL Worth The Cost and What Plan is Best For You?</strong></h2>



<p>If you’re building on <strong>one brand</strong>, Starter keeps costs simple while you validate. For <strong>agencies</strong>, Unlimited’s <strong>unlimited sub‑accounts</strong> is the growth unlock—and with rebilling turned on, usage scales with clients.</p>



<p>If you’re ready to <strong>sell software</strong>, Pro/SaaS adds packaging, markups, and billing so you can price confidently and grow margins. I provide an in-depth <a href="https://passivesecrets.com/gohighlevel-reviews/">review of GoHighLevel</a> based on my personal experience.</p>
